channel.title: Automation on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Automation on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: рҹҡҖ GitHub Actions Certification Guide (GH-200) - Complete Prep
item.description:
Introduction

Welcome to Part 3 of the GitHub Certification Journey! рҹҺҜ

After mastering GitHub Foundations (GH-900) and GitHub Administration (GH-100), you’re ready to dive deep into GitHub Actions - the powerhouse automation platform that transforms how teams build, test, and deploy software. The GH-200 GitHub Actions certification validates your ability to design, implement, and manage CI/CD pipelines at enterprise scale.

This comprehensive guide provides everything needed to pass the GH-200 exam and become a GitHub Actions expert. Whether you’re a DevOps engineer, platform team member, or developer looking to automate workflows, this preparation roadmap will take you from YAML basics to advanced enterprise automation patterns.

item.title: вҡҷпёҸ Automated Updates Pipeline
item.description:
Why this pipeline exists

Manually curating weekly platform updates (Azure service changes, GitHub changelog entries, Terraform provider releases) is repetitive, timeвҖ‘sensitive, and errorвҖ‘prone. This pipeline automates the whole path from data acquisition вҶ’ AI summarisation вҶ’ Hugo markdown generation вҶ’ commit, delivering consistent, timestamped update posts under content/updates.

HighвҖ‘level architecture

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ cron 05:15 UTC в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ grouped items в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ GitHub Action в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ> в”Ӯ PowerShell Script в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ> в”Ӯ AI Summarisation в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
 в”Ӯ commit (if changed) в”Ӯ filtered items в”Ӯ summaries
 V V V
 Repo content <в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ write markdown posts <в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ assemble front matter

Scheduling & cadence

The workflow runs daily at 05:15 UTC but perвҖ‘source publication cadence is controlled inside the script using a frequency map (Azure=weekly,GitHub=weekly,Terraform=weekly by default). The script:

item.title: рҹҡҖ Find Stale Branches Across All GitHub Repos
item.description:
Keeping your GitHub repositories clean is crucial for maintainability. Over time, branches pile up, making it difficult to track whatвҖҷs relevant. If youвҖҷre managing multiple repositories in an organization, manually identifying stale branches can be a hassle.

Luckily, with the GitHub CLI (gh), you can automate this process and generate a report of all non-main branches along with their last authors.

This guide walks you through a Bash script that:
вң… Retrieves all repositories in an organization
вң… Lists branches, excluding main, master, and azure-master
вң… Identifies the last commit author for each branch
вң… Groups the results by author for better visibility
